purpose: Promote the private security bounty report lane from a generic checkpoint game into a lane-specific defensive scope-run module with generated scope-citadel texture, five evidence-derived guard stations, and explicit approval gates.

queries: HackerOne program scope bounty report submission private disclosure dashboard
Bugcrowd vulnerability disclosure report workflow scope triage UI
security bounty platform report submission scope duplicate triage dashboard
design_takeaways: Scope, rules, and program eligibility are the first visual gate for private security bounty work.
Duplicate, stale, rejected, and triage signals should be visible before any private report route is considered.
Testing, browser research, public submission, private disclosure, accounts, and payout-sensitive actions should remain locked until explicit approval.

image_generation.prompt_summary: Premium dark defensive security command chamber with luminous scope walls, private-report vault doors, duplicate sentry checkpoints, static proof tablets, safe review corridors, and sealed testing/submission gates; no people, text, logos, code snippets, exploit payloads, or offensive visuals.

frontend_features: Added scope-run dispatch and step-count support.
Added five data-derived guard stations: Scope Wall, Duplicate Sweep, Static Proof, Private Route, and Approval Gate.
Added a lane-specific board with shield walls, sentinels, approval locks, station cards, and responsive desktop/mobile layouts.
Added versioned cache keys and README documentation for standalone reuse.
safety_boundaries: The module reads local trail/snapshot data only.
It does not perform security testing, exploit reproduction, live browsing, account work, report submission, public disclosure, or payout chasing.
The approval station visualizes those boundaries as locked.
